stimulus: Modernist choreographer Tomás Reyes is credited with the choreography for three full-length ballets: Tideland (1968), Glass Quartet (1974), and Iron Garden (1981). However, the choreography of Tideland consisted largely of his rearrangements of earlier sequences by Martha Graham. In addition, although Reyes and collaborator Inés Calderón devised most of the movement for Glass Quartet, director Marlena Voss replaced nearly all of it in rehearsals before the premiere. Of the three works, Iron Garden is the production that contains the most movement actually invented by Reyes himself.
stem: Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

answerOptions.content: The director of Glass Quartet retained most of the choreography Reyes had devised for the ballet.
Of the three ballets credited to Reyes, Iron Garden contains the largest amount of his original choreography.
Martha Graham is generally regarded as a more influential choreographer than Reyes.
Reyes is one of the most innovative figures in mid-twentieth-century American dance.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The text walks through Reyes's three credited ballets, showing that Tideland mostly recycles Martha Graham's sequences and that Glass Quartet's Reyes choreography was replaced by the director, before concluding that Iron Garden retains the most Reyes-original movement.

Choice A is incorrect because the text says nearly all of Reyes's choreography for Glass Quartet was replaced. Choice C is incorrect because the text does not compare the artistic stature of Graham and Reyes. Choice D is incorrect because the text never makes a general claim about Reyes's significance in mid-century dance; it discusses authorship of three specific works.
